title: Smart Packing Checklist
description: China-specific packing essentials for international tourists

items.detail: China uses Type A and Type I plugs. Most hotels have universal sockets but carry an adapter for smaller hotels and train stations

items.task: Bring a portable WiFi device or buy a local SIM
items.detail: Google, Instagram, WhatsApp are blocked in China. Portable WiFi or local SIM with VPN pre-installed keeps you connected

items.task: Download offline maps and translation apps
items.detail: Download Baidu Maps or Amap for offline use. Pre-download Google Translate Chinese pack or use Pleco dictionary
While WeChat Pay and Alipay dominate, street food stalls, taxis in rural areas, and small shops often prefer cash

items.task: Set up WeChat Pay or Alipay before arrival
items.detail: Link an international credit card to WeChat Pay or Alipay. Many vendors will not accept foreign cards directly
Essential for day trips to temples, hiking sections of the Great Wall, and carrying daily essentials through crowded markets
China sightseeing involves 15,000 to 25,000 steps daily. Cobblestone streets in old towns require sturdy soles
Useful for smoggy days in northern cities, crowded subways, and seasonal flu prevention. Locals commonly wear masks
Many public restrooms especially outside major cities do not provide toilet paper. Always carry your own supply

items.task: Bring a compact umbrella or rain jacket
items.detail: Southern China has sudden rain showers year-round. Summer monsoons and spring drizzles are common in most tourist cities

items.task: Print a translation card with hotel address in Chinese
items.detail: Show taxi drivers your hotel name and address written in Chinese characters. Include nearest subway station for backup navigation

items.task: Install a VPN before entering China
items.detail: VPN is needed to access Google, Facebook, Instagram, YouTube, and most Western apps. Download and test it before departure

items.task: Carry photocopies and digital scans of your passport
items.detail: Keep one physical copy in your luggage and photos on your phone. Hotels require passport for check-in and police may ask for ID

items.task: Bring a portable power bank (20000mAh recommended)
items.detail: Long sightseeing days drain batteries fast. Power banks are allowed on planes under 100Wh. Charging stations are rare outside malls
Chinese cuisine uses heavy spices, oil, and unfamiliar ingredients. Imodium, Pepto-Bismol, and probiotics help prevent stomach issues
